rationale: The pair has only a bare DOI match (no citation context text), so there is limited direct evidence of how the 2018 paper is used in the 2019 paper. The topics overlap on household scanner data and bulk/storable purchasing behavior, but the cited study’s core focus (warehouse-club shopping effects on packaged-food quantity/nutrition) is not obviously the same as the citing paper’s core liquidity-constraint identification question. Given this mixed but plausible relevance, it is not safe to drop as an obvious mention-only citation.
